text: Update `README.md` to document the new `~/.apiease` environment-selection and env-file authentication flow.
goal: introduce a reusable home-configuration layer in the Node client so apiease-cli reads the active environment from ~/.apiease/environment, validates that it is local, staging, or production, loads the matching ~/.apiease/.env.<environment> file, resolves APIEASE_API_KEY from that file, and uses it whenever --api-key is not explicitly passed. Keep explicit CLI flags as the highest-precedence overrides, keep the bash wrapper thin, fail fast with clear structured errors when the environment declaration is missing or invalid or when the selected env file is missing or lacks APIEASE_API_KEY, and update tests and README so the new ~/.apiease environment-selection and env-file authentication flow is fully covered and documented.
context: The goal explicitly requires the README to cover the new setup and precedence rules.
steps: Document the purpose and allowed values of `~/.apiease/environment`.
Document the expected `~/.apiease/.env.<environment>` files and required `APIEASE_API_KEY` entry.
Document the precedence rule that explicit `--api-key` overrides the home-configured key.
constraints: Keep the README aligned with the implemented Node-client behavior.
Document the user-facing failure cases they must correct when configuration is missing or invalid.
acceptanceCriteria: The README shows how to configure `local`, `staging`, and `production` in `~/.apiease/environment`.
The README explains where the CLI loads `APIEASE_API_KEY` from when `--api-key` is omitted.
The README explains that `--api-key` remains the highest-precedence override and calls out the key configuration errors users may encounter.
